item.title: Trump admin. imposes new tariffs on dozens of countries over forced labor concerns
item.description: The Trump administration will impose tariffs of up to 12.5% on goods from 60 U.S. trading partners accused of failing to crack down on forced labor, extending the administration's tariffs on much of the world.

item.title: Man shot and killed by police in Madison, Wisconsin, sparking protests
item.description: Madison's chief of police says an officer in Wisconsin's capital city shot and killed a man who had injured him with a knife while resisting arrest.

item.title: Tampa, Charleston airports to join new TSA privatized security initiative
item.description: The new Gold+ initiative would allow private contractors to handle passenger screening at three airports while the TSA retains federal oversight.

item.title: Measles outbreak "slowing," but newborn's family still can't meet her
item.description: Utah's weekly measles case count has fallen. The national tally hasn't, and a newborn who's too young to be vaccinated is caught in between.

item.title: DOJ to withdraw subpoenas for New York Times reporters over Air Force One story
item.description: The New York Times moved to block subpoenas issued by the Justice Department for grand jury testimony and phone records from its reporters.

item.title: Melinda French Gates Shares 3 Things Women Can Do to Better Advocate for Their Health
item.description: On the first episode of "Healthful," Melinda French Gates shared three simple things women can do to better advocate for their health.

item.title: Global oil prices hit $100 a barrel amid attacks in Red Sea
item.description: The last time global oil prices settled above $100 was on May 22, data from FactSet shows.

item.title: Trump talks plan to prevent ratepayers from shouldering costs of new AI data centers
item.description: President Trump spoke Thursday about a plan to prevent ratepayers from taking on the costs of a new AI data centers. CBS News' Kelly O'Grady has more.

item.title: Vance Boelter gets 2 life terms and more in Minnesota lawmaker shootings
item.description: Vance Boelter, the man who admitted in court to shooting two Minnesota lawmakers and their spouses, was sentenced to two life terms in prison followed by 40 years Thursday morning.

item.title: 1.6 million eggs are recalled over potential salmonella contamination
item.description: The recall affects white-shell and cage-free eggs sold under multiple grocery brands in six states. No illnesses have been reported.

item.title: Doctor Shares 3 Ways to Lower Your Risk of Getting Lung Cancer
item.description: Dr. Kim Sandler, the director of the Vanderbilt Lung Screening Program, shared three simple things women can do to lower their risk of lung cancer. Sandler joined "Healthful" with Anne Wojcicki, the former CEO of 23andMe, and Shira Kupperman Boehler.

item.title: Trump threatens Iran and Houthis amid signs of U.S. military buildup
item.description: The U.S. moves more refueling planes to the Mideast and adds medics at a major military hospital as the war with Iran spreads to another key waterway.

item.title: Videos show Tropical Storm Bertha's impact on Gulf Coast
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha made landfall in Louisiana, causing dangerous surf and flooding parts of the coast. Videos captured Bertha's impact in Louisiana and Alabama, and showed how Texas was preparing for the storm.

item.title: GOP's Jack Smith referral fails to show he committed a crime, ex-prosecutors say
item.description: The Justice Department will face an uphill battle if it tries to prosecute Jack Smith over allegations leveled by House Republicans, former prosecutors say.

item.title: How OpenAI models went rogue during a training exercise
item.description: OpenAI is investigating after two of its AI bots went rogue and targeted an outside company during a training exercise in what the company called an "unprecedented cyber incident." Ian Krietzberg, an AI correspondent at Puck, joins with more.

item.title: Ukraine strikes warehouses of top Russian online retailer Wildberries, harms logistics capacity
item.description: Since the weekend, Ukraine has struck four warehouses belonging to leading Russian retailer Wildberries, often described as the country's Amazon. Aidan Stretch tells the story from Kyiv.

item.title: 2 missing in flash floods that inundated West Virginia
item.description: Torrential rains in West Virginia have caused flash flooding that inundated homes and businesses this week.

item.title: Were the deaths of a Pennsylvania pastor's two wives coincidence or murder?
item.description: An apparent suicide at a small-town church exposes secrets, betrayal and questions about two more deaths

item.title: Watch: Leavitt takes questions on U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal, China's AI tech, New Jersey voter rolls
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt answered questions from reporters Thursday on a number of topics, including the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal, China's artificial intelligence technology and more. Following the briefing, CBS News' Aaron Navarro shared his takeaways.

item.title: Senate Judiciary Committee prepares to vote on Todd Blanche's nomination for Attorney General
item.description: The Senate Judiciary Committee is set to vote on Todd Blanche's nomination for attorney general on Thursday as bipartisan concerns remain about his recent leadership under President Trump. Taurean Small shares what to expect and dives into the failed Senate effort to direct Mr. Trump to end the Iran war.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha moves inland near Texas-Louisiana border
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season.

item.title: NYPD says dozens rescued from human trafficking during World Cup
item.description: There were 89 people arrested in the New York area over the course of the World Cup, the NYPD said.

item.title: Fewer Americans own homes than thought, new Fed search finds
item.description: Only about 53% of Americans own homes, or about 12 percentage points lower than previous data has shown, the study found.

item.title: Tilly Norwood's creator insists the AI actor is creating Hollywood jobs
item.description: The robot actor is generating work for Hollywood creatives, not stealing jobs from actors, her inventor tells CBS News.

item.title: FDA says new cyclospora outbreak linked to unspecified product
item.description: Federal health officials said they've identified a new outbreak of the cyclospora parasite, but the source hasn't been specified.

item.title: Middle East oil producers step up plans to bypass the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: As Iran's attacks keep ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz to a trickle and oil prices surge, Gulf states are planning to invest billions in pipeline alternatives.

item.title: Member of Vance's Secret Service detail under investigation for leaking
item.description: The Secret Service said a member of Vice President JD Vance's detail is under investigation for "compromising operational and information security."

item.title: Melinda French Gates Explains Why She Tracks Her Blood Work in a Spreadsheet
item.description: Melinda French Gates told Norah O'Donnell on "Healthful" that she's been tracking all her blood work in a spreadsheet "for a very long time." "It's easier than when I sit down with my doctor and she's pacing through the computer to look at it. I'm like, here's the spreadsheet," French Gates said.

item.title: Mexican man killed by truck after ICE encounter was on vacation, officials say
item.description: Juan Jairo Coronilla Durán, 28, died July 14 after authorities said he darted across a busy road in St. Augustine, Florida, and into the path of a truck.

item.title: My Mother's Murder
item.description: A teenager's mother is murdered more than a year after her father met the same fate. Were the deaths connected? Richard Schlesinger reports.
